title: Vietnam with Kids — The Honest NZ Family Guide (2026)
meta_desc: Vietnam family holiday guide from NZ. Ha Long Bay, Hoi An, Hanoi with kids — real costs, what works, what doesn't, and the flight options from Auckland.
tagline: History, street food, and Ha Long Bay — unforgettable at any age
hero_alt: Ha Long Bay limestone islands and emerald water, Vietnam
flight_from_nz: 11–14 hours (Auckland to Hanoi or Ho Chi Minh City, usually 1 stop)
intro: Vietnam is one of the most rewarding family destinations in Asia — a country of spectacular landscapes, 4,000 years of history, extraordinary food, and near-Bali prices. Ha Long Bay is genuinely one of the world's great travel experiences. It takes more planning than Bali but rewards families with richer, more memorable experiences.
verdict: Excellent for families with kids 6+. Best suited to families who enjoy history and culture alongside beaches. Best done with a local guide or organised itinerary.

best_destinations.name: Ha Long Bay
best_destinations.tagline: One of the world's seven natural wonders — absolutely unmissable
best_destinations.why: Ha Long Bay is the highlight of any Vietnam trip. 1,600 limestone islands rising from jade-green water — unlike anything else on earth. Book a 2-night cruise on a junk boat (from NZ$250/adult, NZ$160/child). Kids love the kayaking, the floating villages, and sleeping in the cabin as the boat moves.
must_do: 2-night junk boat cruise (book through reputable operator — avoid bottom-budget boats)
best_destinations.cost: Budget NZ$250–400/day family of 4 during cruise (all-inclusive)

best_destinations.name: Hoi An
best_destinations.tagline: The most beautiful town in Vietnam — lanterns, tailors, and ancient streets
best_destinations.why: Hoi An's ancient town is a UNESCO World Heritage site — and genuinely magical at night when thousands of lanterns are lit. The beach (An Bang Beach, 4km away) is excellent. Tailors can make custom kids' clothing overnight for $15–25. The town is compact, car-free in the centre, and very walkable with kids.
must_do: Ancient town by night (lantern festival on the 14th of each month)
Cooking class — Morning Glory in the market is excellent for families
An Bang Beach (safe swimming, great cafes)
Make your own lantern activity ($5 per person)

best_destinations.name: Hanoi
best_destinations.tagline: Chaotic, historic, and fascinating — the beating heart of Vietnam
best_destinations.why: Hanoi is overwhelming at first but incredibly rewarding. The Old Quarter is a living museum — 36 ancient street guilds, each specialising in one trade. Ho Chi Minh's mausoleum, Hoan Kiem Lake, and the Vietnam Museum of Ethnology are all world-class. Best for families with kids 8+ who can handle the chaos.
must_do: Hoan Kiem Lake and Ngoc Son Temple (walk the red bridge to the island)
Vietnam Museum of Ethnology (NZ$5 entry, extraordinary)
Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum complex (free, arrive early)
Old Quarter walking tour with a guide
Water puppet show ($10 — kids love it)

practical_info.currency: Vietnamese Dong (VND). NZ$1 ≈ 14,500 VND (2026). Use ATMs only — never street money changers.
practical_info.health: Hep A, typhoid, and Japanese encephalitis vaccines recommended for rural areas. Mosquito repellent essential. Stick to cooked food or well-known restaurants.
practical_info.food_safety: Pho, banh mi, and fresh spring rolls from busy local restaurants are generally safe. Avoid ice in street stalls. Look for places with high turnover.
practical_info.sim_card: Viettel or Mobifone SIM at the airport. NZ$10 for 30 days unlimited data.
practical_info.visa: NZ passport holders need an e-visa (US$25, apply online at evisa.xuatnhapcanh.gov.vn). Takes 3 business days.
practical_info.transport: Domestic flights on VietJet or Bamboo Airways are very cheap ($20–60 per person). Night train Hanoi–Da Nang is an adventure with kids. Grab (like Uber) for all city transport.

budget_breakdown.food: NZ$20–50/day eating local — Vietnam has some of the world's best cheap food
budget_breakdown.transport: NZ$20–60/day including domestic flights if needed

nz_affiliate_note: No direct flights Auckland–Vietnam. Best options: Air NZ via Singapore, China Southern via Guangzhou, or Vietnam Airlines via Sydney. Book 3+ months ahead.
